note: Verifies Astro 7 compiler behavior, GFM markdown built-ins, and the agent-native background dev-server lifecycle. Not a timing benchmark. No statistical distribution applies: each check is a deterministic pass/fail observation for astro@7.0.6. source_of_truth = probe-result.json (a fresh 2026-07-20 execution).
subject: astro 7.0.6 (Rust compiler, Vite 8 / Rolldown)

behaviors_verified.claim: Astro 7's Rust compiler collapses insignificant JSX-style whitespace: a newline+indent between two adjacent <span> elements produces NO visible space.
behaviors_verified.old_behavior_cited_not_measured: Astro 6's Go compiler preserved the whitespace ('Hello World'). This regression contrast is cited from the migration guide, not executed here.

behaviors_verified.claim:
An unterminated

tag is a HARD compiler error under Astro 7 — the build fails with exit 1 instead of silently auto-closing.
behaviors_verified.observed: build_exit_code=1; '[CompilerError] Expected corresponding JSX closing tag for p'; stacktrace routes through rolldown (confirms Vite 8 / Rolldown backend).
behaviors_verified.old_behavior_cited_not_measured: Astro 6 silently inserted the missing closing tag. Cited, not measured.

behaviors_verified.claim: Astro 7 ships an agent-native background dev server: `astro dev --background` detaches and reports a pid, `astro dev status` reports it running in background, a machine-readable lockfile (.astro/dev.json) tracks pid/port/url, and `astro dev stop` when nothing is running exits 0 (idempotent).
behaviors_verified.observed: start reports pid; status reports 'background'; .astro/dev.json lockfile present; second stop => 'No dev server is running.' exit 0.
behaviors_verified.note: CLI surface confirmed: subcommands stop/status/logs[--follow] + flag --background. Logs are NDJSON. The non-TTY auto-background behavior noted in the post is documented but not asserted by this automated probe.

behaviors_verified.claim: GFM markdown (tables, strikethrough, footnotes, task lists) renders out of the box with ZERO remark/rehype plugins installed, via the Rust markdown pipeline (Sätteri = pulldown-cmark + Oxc).
behaviors_verified.observed: dist/post/index.html contains <table>, <del>, footnote refs, and type=checkbox task items — no plugins configured.

cited_not_measured.claim: Rust compiler alone accounts for ~6% of build-time improvement; the larger 15-61% headline gains are mostly Vite 8 / Rolldown.
cited_not_measured.note: Build times are machine-dependent and non-deterministic, so they are excluded from firsthand evidence entirely.

cited_not_measured.claim: Astro 6's Go compiler preserved JSX whitespace and auto-closed unterminated tags.
cited_not_measured.note: Astro 6 was not installed; the before/after contrast is cited.

honest_limitations: Only the Astro 7 side is executed. Every 'old behavior' contrast is cited, not measured.
Whitespace/HTML-correction breaking changes can cause render regressions in existing projects (a migration hazard, not a bug).
Build/install timing is deliberately not part of the evidence — the harness records pass/fail behavior only.
